prompt.content:
I want to determine percentage of gross profit contributed by top customers in 2021, 2022 and 2023.

Top customers are defined for each year independently as customers with a Z-score greater than one, where Z-scores are calculated based on net revenue among customers with non-zero revenue in that year. Customers contributing 0 revenue in the year should be ignored for that year's analysis. 

Assume the COGS as percentage of Net revenue is 1 pp lower for top customers compared to remaining customers in that year. Let's use sample standard deviation when calculating Z-scores please. 

Use data in Deliverable 1_Audited Redwood Components Tax Exposure.xlsx and attached Customer_Net_Revenue.pdf for this analysis.

Do not round intermediate calculations. Round final values to two decimal places and report them in the console.

rubrics.name: States the percentage of gross profit contributed by top customers (defined as... (ver_742be4a388624ccfa11b12ad5d65770b)

messages.content: States the percentage of gross profit contributed by top customers (defined as those with a Z-score greater than one) for 2021 is 42.12% (acceptable range 42.11% to 42.13%).

rubrics.name: States the percentage of gross profit contributed by top customers (defined as... (ver_d7bf7820b5754566acd687a14567abb6)

messages.content: States the percentage of gross profit contributed by top customers (defined as those with a Z-score greater than one) in 2022 is 38.66% (acceptable range 38.65% to 38.67%).

rubrics.name: States the percentage of gross profit contributed by top customers (defined as... (ver_fd06b40030f34e348be2dc31aee85591)

messages.content: States the percentage of gross profit contributed by top customers (defined as those with a Z-score greater than one) in 2023 is 40.18% (acceptable range 40.17% to 40.19%).

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
Based on the analysis of the audited Redwood Components Tax Exposure and the customer-wise net revenue data, the percentage of gross profit contributed by top customers (defined as those with a Z-score greater than one) is as follows:

2021: 42.12%
2022: 38.66%
2023: 40.18%
